unresolved_mentions: The filing references 'two parties engaged in the Company’s industry' in 2007 and 2009, but they are unnamed and cannot be linked to later parties.
SEACOR appears both as an activist shareholder and as a potential bidder; treated as separate role entries for the same named entity.
Party G was considered as a possible counterparty but not contacted; bidder participation is limited to board consideration in the market check discussion.
The text notes categories of 'financial sponsors and strategic purchasers' but does not provide counts for financial sponsors, so no grouped financial-buyer actor was created.
Prospect Sub, Inc. is named in merger agreement references but is not a bidder, advisor, activist, or target board under the requested schema.
